title: Different 3rd Impacts
wikitext:
[]: Different 3rd Impacts?             Quote Post by feliwood » Sat Sep 18, 2004 3:37 pm I don't understand the difference between Gendo's ideal version of the 3rd impact, Seele's ideal version of the 3rd impact, and the Angel's ideal version of the 3rd impact. It seems that, no matter what happens, Gendo would be with Yui, the human race would be evolved, and the Angels will have acchieved what they all seem bound to do, and everyone gets what they want. Of course, if this was the case, there would be no conflict in the series, but of course, there is conflict, so it seems that, if Gendo gets what he wants, then Keil doesnt get what he wants. What, then, is the difference between these different versions of the 3rd impact?

[]:             Quote Post by Phaze » Sat Sep 18, 2004 4:41 pm From what I understand, Gendo simply meant to stop Third Impact before the rest of humanity was complemented, so no, there's only one Third Impact.

[]:             Quote Post by feliwood » Sat Sep 18, 2004 8:19 pm You mean he would start the whole thing, merge with Yui, and then stop before anyone else was added to the mix? What has he said/done that points to that?

[]:             Quote Post by thewayneiac » Sat Sep 18, 2004 8:59 pm feliwood wrote: You mean he would start the whole thing, merge with Yui, and then stop before anyone else was added to the mix? What has he said/done that points to that?

[]:             Quote Post by sadsadshinji » Sat Sep 18, 2004 9:28 pm the different third impacts? i agree with thewayneiac but i think that perhaps seele's scenario would have gone even farther than was shown (the evolution that they were speaking of might have happened after the lillith/uber-rei part)

[]:             Quote Post by Tsukasa » Thu Dec 16, 2004 7:44 pm thewayneiac is correct. There are indeed 3 different 3rd Impacts that could have taken place. 1. Gendou's 3rd Impact: In EoE, when Rei betrays Gendou and enter Lilith alone, that was the plug pulled on Gendou's plan as he only wanted to be with Yui again. Rei then proceeded to orchestrate the "good" 3rd Impact. 2. Seele's 3rd Impact: This was what began happening, except Shinji pulled decided "Nah." Mankind returns to one being, complimenting eachother making sadness, lonliness and emptiness cease to exist. But, since Shinji pulled the plug, mankind ended up as it began, Asuka and Shinji (Adam and Eve). 3. Angel's 3rd Impact: This would have happened if mankind lost to the Angels. Humans (although being Angels themselves) would have been eradicated and the Earth left for the Angels to live upon. All of these are just simple explanations, keeping it understandable. ; )

[]:             Quote Post by Reichu » Thu Dec 16, 2004 9:09 pm Tsukasa wrote: But, since Shinji pulled the plug, mankind ended up as it began, Asuka and Shinji (Adam and Eve).

[]:             Quote Post by Tsukasa » Fri Dec 17, 2004 2:25 am I do not understand how Shinji and Asuka representing Adam and Eve is a misconception. Now I must admit that I have not devoted my entire existence to this anime (my life is with God), but that is always how I've seen it. Please explain why it isn't valid.

[]:             Quote Post by mike » Fri Dec 17, 2004 8:34 am Well, for starters, they aren't Adam and Eve because anybody with the will to live on survived Third Impact, thus not making them the only people alive in the world.

[]:             Quote Post by bp32 » Fri Dec 17, 2004 8:46 am mike wrote: Well, for starters, they aren't Adam and Eve because anybody with the will to live on survived Third Impact, thus not making them the only people alive in the world.

[]:             Quote Post by Reichu » Fri Dec 17, 2004 9:14 am Some links on the ability of ALL PEOPLE to return (recommended reading, BTW, even if some of the discussion is "off-topic"): http://www.animenation.net/forums/showt ... p?t=153270 http://animenation.net/forums/showthread.php?t=113648 http://animenation.net/forums/showthrea ... ge=3&pp=15

[]:             Quote Post by Tsukasa » Fri Dec 17, 2004 2:41 pm AHH! I see now. Thank you for clearing that up for me. I have forgotten about that bit of information given by Yui and Rei.

[]:             Quote Post by Reichu » Fri Dec 17, 2004 4:49 pm It wasn't the only indication that people were going to return, either.

[]:             Quote Post by Tsukasa » Fri Dec 17, 2004 6:02 pm <--- Needs to go and watch the Evangelion movies/series again for the 12,493 time....

[]:             Quote Post by DatDude » Wed Dec 22, 2004 9:43 am Just to throw in my two cents I feel that gendo's impact would have been pretty much what happened in EoE with him in control rather then Shinji. Once GEndogot what he wanted " to be with yui again" I don't think he'd have returned to the real world, leaving humantiy stuck in instrumentality. Remember its very in character for gendo to screw other people to get what he wants in this case all of humanity.

[]:             Quote Post by ChronoX » Wed Dec 22, 2004 12:12 pm I always thought that if the lilium lost to an angel, they would be absorbed into that angel or become that angel. Not that if that lilium lost to an angel that all lilium would be erased from exsistance dosen't make sense either.

[]:             Quote Post by Reichu » Wed Dec 22, 2004 2:50 pm ChronoX wrote: I always thought that if the lilium lost to an angel, they would be absorbed into that angel or become that angel. Not that if that lilium lost to an angel that all lilium would be erased from exsistance dosen't make sense either.

[]:             Quote Post by ChronoX » Thu Dec 23, 2004 4:41 pm Ok, i get it. What about the Anti AT field, how does that work?

[]:             Quote Post by bp32 » Thu Dec 23, 2004 5:02 pm ChronoX wrote: Ok, i get it. What about the Anti AT field, how does that work?

[]:             Quote Post by ChronoX » Thu Dec 23, 2004 5:57 pm That much i do know, but i would like to know how, if anyone has any idea.
